issue: Vendor account group (LFA1.KTOKK) and reconciliation account (LFB1.AKONT) drift from the canonical mapping in T077K. Discovered case: vendor 10133079 (Katja HINZ) has KTOKK=SCSA â†’ AKONT=2021011 in IIEP company code, while T077K canonical mapping for SCSA staff expects AKONT=2021010 (and the GGB1 rule set only covers 2021042 for KTOKK=UNES). Class of defect, not a one-off â€” full vendor master never audited for KTOKKâ†”AKONT consistency.
impact: Caused INC-000006073 (Travel posting RW609/ZFI020 for cross-funded IIEPâ†”UNES trips). Class effect: any vendor with non-canonical AKONT silently passes posting until an intercompany scenario or new GGB1 rule set exposes the gap. UNESCO has 316K vendors in LFA1; total exposure unquantified.
workaround: (a) Run vendor_master_integrity_check.py against Gold DB to list all LFB1 rows where AKONT does not match T077K canonical mapping for the LFA1.KTOKK. (b) For each violation, decide whether to correct AKONT (FK02) or extend GGB1 substitution (YFI_BASU_MOD). (c) Promote this to a recurring monthly check.
affected_count_note: Full LFA1×T077K scan not yet run — tracked as KU-020
resolution_path: Promoted to recurring check Zagentexecution/quality_checks/vendor_master_integrity_check.py (s#050). Runs weekly against Gold DB. FALS-006 predicts ≥5 new drifters on first full scan.

issue: Country-level PPC config codes are UNESCO-proprietary format, not ISO 20022. Documentation PDFs show wrong codes (001/002/003) instead of real (slash-notation).
impact: Payment configuration misunderstood. PDF documentation unusable for PPC mapping.
workaround: Use live T015L extraction. Ignore PDF documentation for PPC codes.
affected_count_note: 47 distinct UNESCO PPC codes in T015L (proprietary format, not ISO 20022)
resolution_path: Documented in payment_bcm_companion.html + epiuse_companion.html. PurposeCode source = FPAYP-XREF3 via BAdI FI_CGI_DMEE_EXIT_W_BADI (see claim #5). No migration planned — UNESCO uses proprietary codes intentionally.

issue: GJAHR=0000 on 119K delivery notes (VGABE=9). Not real GL years but ACH returns marked as 'delivery notes'.
impact: EKBE year filtering problematic. 119K phantom 'year 0000' records exist.
workaround: Filter GJAHR=0000 OUT of delivery note analysis queries.
affected_count_note: 119K EKBE delivery notes with GJAHR=0000 (VGABE=9)
resolution_path: H6 closure (s#028): GJAHR=0000 rows are ACH returns marked as delivery notes. Skipped in BUDAT enrichment. Documented in Payment process mining.

issue: Auth-restricted on P01. Cannot extract via RFC without buffer overflow.
impact: GR field analysis incomplete. Cannot verify item quantities in GR chain.
workaround: Query MEINS via different path or skip in bulk extractions.
affected_count_note: Auth-restricted tables — count unknown without SAP Basis escalation
resolution_path: Document per-table in extraction log. Some tables extractable via chunked WHERE clauses + DDIF_FIELDINFO_GET introspection. FMAVCT handled this way (s#053 retro).

issue: 510 (later 1,690) GL descriptions inconsistent between P01 and D01. P01 has CLOSED-BK prefixes; D01 still has old active names. Text updates inconsistent.
impact: Master data sync incomplete until Session #038 ran multi-language sync (1,511 UPDATEs + 179 INSERTs).
workaround: Use sap_master_data_sync skill pattern: SELECT SINGLE + UPDATE FROM ls (avoids 72-char ABAP truncation).
affected_count_note: 1,690 SKAT rows resolved (518 E + 500 F + 493 P languages + 179 new INSERTs)
resolution_path: H29 closure (s#038): sync script rfc_helpers.py with SELECT SINGLE + UPDATE FROM ls pattern. All 1,690 rows UPDATE ok=1690/ko=0. Log: knowledge/domains/FI/h29_skat_sync_log.md.

issue: All 449K BSAS items have blank AUGBL initially. Clearing chain cannot be traced via AUGBL.
impact: Clearing relationships hidden. Session #030 re-enrichment fixed 553K items with AUGBL+AUGDT (100% fill).
workaround: Use BSAS_2024_2026 enriched table, not raw BSAS extraction.
affected_count_note: 553,781 BSAS items re-enriched with AUGBL+AUGDT
resolution_path: H20 closure (s#030): 100% fill rate via targeted enrichment. Documented in bank_statement_ebs_architecture.md.

issue: Only 8 of 62 DD03L fields extracted initially. BUKRS, HBKID, HKTID missing.
impact: Cannot link statements to banks without FEBKO_2024_2026 enriched table.
workaround: Always query FEBKO_2024_2026 (31,416 rows, 62 fields), not unsuffixed FEBKO.
affected_count_note: 62 DD03L fields (incl. BUKRS/HBKID/HKTID)
resolution_path: Re-extracted with explicit FIELDS list (not default 8). Pattern now standard.

issue: Only 8 of 104 fields extracted initially. Critical fields missing: VGEXT, VGINT, GSBER, ZUONR, INTAG, XBLNR, BUTXT.
impact: EBS analysis incomplete. Real data is in FEBEP_2024_2026 (223K rows, 27 fields).
workaround: Always query FEBEP_2024_2026, not unsuffixed FEBEP (50K rows, 104 cols â€” truncated first attempt).
affected_count_note: 104 FEBEP fields including VGEXT/VGINT/GSBER/ZUONR/INTAG/XBLNR/BUTXT
resolution_path: H22 closure (s#042): FEBEP_2024_2026 table has all 27 critical fields filled. Pattern: always check _2024_2026 suffix, not unsuffixed FEBEP.

issue: TABKEY field contains language keys (D/E/F/P = Deutsch/English/FranÃ§ais/Portuguese), looks like functional types.
impact: Configuration misinterpreted. Wrong analysis in companion before Session #031 correction.
workaround: Apply feedback_tabkey_language_codes rule: D/E/F/P in SAP are ALWAYS language codes, not functional types.
affected_count_note: 4 language keys (D/E/F/P)
resolution_path: Superseded claim #19 (s#031): these are LANGUAGE keys, not period types. No fix needed — documentation correction only.

issue: Script filter missing ICBA and UIL FM areas. Only 7 of 9 institutes included initially. Zero postings for ICBA/UIL appeared as data finding (was extraction bug).
impact: Company code analysis wrong. Shared with team before verification (credibility hit).
workaround: Always verify filter completeness against T001 BUKRS list before publishing findings.
affected_count_note: 2 FM areas missing (ICBA + UIL)
resolution_path: Filter list extended. Re-extraction completed. All 9 institutes now included.

issue: 2,056 IBC17 items all from 2021-2022 BCM outage (Jul2021-Dec2022). No failures 2024-2026.
impact: Stale error state. BCM failure analysis weighted wrong if date range not checked.
workaround: Always filter BCM data to 2024-2026 for current-state analysis.
affected_count_note: 2,056 IBC17 items, all 2021-2022 BCM outage
resolution_path: H16 closure (s#028): out of data scope (2024-2026). Historical record only. Root cause documented: BCM activation Jul2021 misconfigured until Oct2022.

issue: 213 of 219 non-USD bank accounts (3% coverage) + 48 of 204 non-USD clearing accounts (76% coverage) missing OBA1/T030H entries.
impact: FAGL_FC_VAL skips these accounts. FX gains/losses on 80+ currencies at 80+ field offices not reflected in financial statements. Material weakness.
workaround: Requires remediation project. No workaround â€” structural config gap.
affected_count_note: 213 non-USD bank accts + 48 non-USD clearing accts missing T030H entries
resolution_path: Tracked as KU-010 (does FAGL_FC_VAL use fallback?). Needs P01 config check before deciding fix path. May not be a bug — could be intentional (USD-only valuation).

issue: YCL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_AE and YCL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_BH classes don't exist in P01 TADIR. Only in D01 CTS. P01 uses UTIL fallback.
impact: AE and BH countries get default processing instead of custom logic. Configuration incomplete in production.
workaround: Transport the classes to P01 if custom AE/BH logic needed. Currently UTIL fallback is working.
affected_count_note: 2 classes (YCL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_AE + YCL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_BH) exist in D01 TADIR but not P01
resolution_path: Superseded claim #22 (s#039, H18 closure): PurposeCode comes from FPAYP-XREF3 via DMEE tree + BAdI FI_CGI_DMEE_EXIT_W_BADI, not from AE/BH classes. P01 uses UTIL fallback. No migration needed.

issue: SAP clears E071/E071K after transport release. Previous extraction is source of truth. Re-extraction of released transports returns empty.
impact: Can permanently destroy transport content data by re-extracting. Happened in Session #031 (destroyed 36/682, 9/51).
workaround: NEVER re-extract released transports. Check status before extraction. Rule in feedback_never_reextract_released_transports.
affected_count_note: Applies to all released transports — structural SAP behavior
resolution_path: Documented as extraction pattern: always extract transport contents BEFORE release. Previous extraction is source of truth.

issue: 199K 'open' items on 10xxxxx are actually permanent ledger entries (never cleared by design). Not a reconciliation gap.
impact: Misinterpreted as operational crisis for 4 sessions. Real unreconciled = 2,737 on 11xxxxx (0.6%).
workaround: Filter BSIS to 11xxxxx accounts only for reconciliation analysis.
affected_count_note: 199K permanent ledger items on 10xxxxx GLs (by design)
resolution_path: Superseded claim #18 (s#029): these are permanent ledger entries, never cleared. Real unreconciled = 2,737 on 11xxxxx (0.6% gap). H28 closure documented in bank_statement_ebs_architecture.md.

issue: Memory files got Windows-1252 encoding bytes injected during Python stdout writes on Windows, corrupting em dashes and arrows.
impact: feedback_rules.json became unreadable (UTF-8 decode errors) mid-Session #049, required cleanup.
workaround: Always use explicit encoding='utf-8' when reading/writing files. Rule in feedback_always_utf8_encoding.
affected_count_note: Multiple memory files on Windows stdout path
resolution_path: Pattern: always use open(..., encoding='utf-8') + sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding='utf-8') on Windows. Python scripts retrofitted.

issue: 187,514 rows in fmifiit_full (UNES company code, bank clearing GLs 0006045011/0007045011/0006045014) have FISTL='UNESCO' due to the ZXFMDTU02_RPY.abap:99 hardcode. Zero rows on these GLs have FISTL='JAK' or any other field-office FICTR. This makes office-level cash-flow reporting impossible for the bank side of vendor payments.
resolution_path: Apply Option-A SU3-based fix in ZXFMDTU02_RPY:94-101 (see knowledge/incidents/INC-000005240_f53_fistl_unesco_default.md section 6.2) then run Zagentexecution/quality_checks/fmderive_hardcoded_fictr_check.py monthly to confirm the ratio of default-vs-home-FICTR trends toward the home-FICTR side.
affected_count_note: 187,514 fmifiit_full rows UNES + bank clearing GLs + FISTL='UNESCO' hardcode signature

issue: AL_JONATHAN (Jakarta Field Unit user) has USR05 SU3 parameter Y_USERFO='HQ' instead of 'JAK'. Every F-53/FBZ2 posting he makes carries BSEG-XREF1='HQ' BSEG-XREF2='HQ' instead of the correct 'JAK'. PA0001.WERKS='ID00' BTRTL='JKT' confirms HR has him assigned to Indonesia/Jakarta.
impact: 10 documents in 2026-02/03 carry wrong XREF tag. No automated detection. Manual correction via FBL3N by A_HIZKIA on 2 of 10.
status: OPEN - fix pending SU3 update after KU-027 YFO_CODES.JAK verification
affected_count_note: 1 user (AL_JONATHAN) with Y_USERFO='HQ' instead of 'JAK'
resolution_path: H45 (s#054). Blocked by KU-027 (verify YFO_CODES has FOCOD='JAK') before SU3 update. See knowledge/incidents/INC-000005240_xref_office_substitution.md.

issue: Systemic XREF drift at UNESCO: 21,754 manual post-posting edits via FBL3N/FBL1N/FB02/FBL5N in Q1 2026 on UNES BELEG documents, across 242 distinct users, touching 24,597 distinct documents. This is the operational workaround for missing XREF validation and signals a systemic gap rather than isolated errors.
impact: Ongoing operational burden. Users spend significant time manually correcting XREF values because no runtime validation catches wrong values.
status: OPEN - strategic fix options in Treasury xref_office_tagging_model.md §7.3
affected_count_note: 21,754 manual XREF edits Q1 2026 via FBL3N/FBL1N/FB02/FBL5N on UNES BELEG documents
resolution_path: H46 (s#054). Strategic fix options in knowledge/domains/Treasury/xref_office_tagging_model.md §7.3. Decision pending between preventive substitution fix / opt-in SU3 model / periodic reconciliation / tolerate. Blocked by H48.

issue: USR05.Y_USERFO (finance office code) and PA0001.BTRTL (HR personnel subarea) use different code systems at UNESCO with no enforced mapping. Example drift: Kabul is BTRTL='KBL' but Y_USERFO='KAB'; Dakar is BTRTL='DKR' but Y_USERFO='DAK'. A user's office in finance can drift from their HR assignment with no automated detection.
source: Live RFC 2026-04-09 PA0001 + USR05 for 7 users
impact: Enables the DQ-018 class of issues to persist undetected. A user can be hired in Jakarta (HR correct) and still have wrong finance office code (USR05) forever.
affected_count_note: Unknown drift count without full USR05×PA0001 diff — tracked as KU-028
resolution_path: H47 (s#054). Organizational process gap. Needs HR→BASIS alert mechanism + periodic diff report + policy for mandatory SU3 update on HR transfer. Cross-domain item.

issue: Ghost PERNR 10567156 active in UIS BCM rules 90000004 + 90000005 since 2025-10-04. PA0105/0001 empty -> workflow 90000003 cannot route BNK_APP work items -> Svein OESTTVEIT silently cannot sign UIS payments even though he appears on the Citibank Canada carton des signatures as PERNR 10067156.
impact: Production payment routing for UIS is understaffed by one committer. If Svein would have been the deciding signatory, the batch waits or a different user approves. The real PERNR 10067156 (user S_OESTTVEIT, email S.OESTTVEIT@UNESCO.ORG) should have been used in OOCU_RESP.
workaround: None automated. DBS must delimit HRP1001 SOBID=10567156 and insert fresh rows with SOBID=10067156 after TRS authorization. Until then, the other 7 UIS signatories on the carton must cover Svein's work.
resolution: DBS delimited HRP1001 SOBID=10567156 on RY 50010054 + RY 50036801 (ENDDA set pre-20260413), inserted new rows with SOBID=10067156 (real Svein OESTTVEIT, user S_OESTTVEIT, BEGDA=20260413 ENDDA=99991231). Verified 2026-04-13 via RFC_READ_TABLE: 0 ghosts remain. Carton diff exit=0.
affected_count_note: 1 ghost PERNR (10567156 / Svein OESTTVEIT) active in 2 BCM rules
resolution_path: INC-000006313 closure (s#052). Reconciliation check Zagentexecution/quality_checks/bcm_signatory_reconciliation_check.py added as recurring detector. PERNR removed from rules.

issue: KU-011 hypothesised T042Z extraction missing DMEE_ASSIGNMENT_TABLE column. Verified Session #63 (2026-04-30) via Gold DB PRAGMA table_info(T042Z): T042Z has 35 cols, no DMEE_ASSIGNMENT_TABLE column, but FORMI / XBKKT / XSTRA all present. The hypothetical missing column does not exist in standard SAP T042Z schema — KU-011 was based on incorrect schema assumption.
resolution: CLOSED. T042Z fully extracted (263 rows). Per-entity differentiation via T042A (ZBUKR + ZLSCH + WAERS → HBKID).
